content.problemStatement:
You are given arrays AAA and BBB with NNN non-negative integers each.

An array XXX of length NNN is called good, if:


All elements of the array XXX are non-negative;
X1X_1X1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ X2X_2X2‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶ ‚ą£|‚ą£ Xi=AiX_i = A_iXi‚Äč=Ai‚Äč for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1\le i\le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N);
XiX_iXi‚Äč &\&& X(i+1)X_{(i+1)}X(i+1)‚Äč &\&& ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶ &\&& XN=BiX_N = B_iXN‚Äč=Bi‚Äč for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1\le i\le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N).
Find the **maximum** bitwise XOR of all elements over all good arrays XXX.

More formally, find the maximum value of X1‚äēX2‚äē‚Ä¶XNX_1\oplus X_2\oplus \ldots X_NX1‚Äč‚äēX2‚Äč‚äē‚Ä¶XN‚Äč, over all good arrays XXX.

It is guaranteed that at least one such array XXX exists.

Note that ‚ą£,&,|, \&,‚ą£,&, and ‚äē\oplus‚äē denote the bitwise or, and, and xor operations respectively.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers describing the elements of the array AAA.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers describing the elements of the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** bitwise XOR of all elements over all good arrays XXX.
constraints: It is guaranteed that at least one such array XXX exists.
Sum of NNN over all test cases is less than 3‚čÖ1053\cdot 10^{5}3‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: An optimal good array is X=[0,3,2]X = [0, 3, 2]X=[0,3,2].

For i=1i = 1i=1: A1=X1=0A_1 = X_1 = 0A1‚Äč=X1‚Äč=0 and B1=X1B_1 = X_1B1‚Äč=X1‚Äč &\&& X2X_2X2‚Äč &\&& X3=0X_3 = 0X3‚Äč=0.
For i=2i = 2i=2: A2=X1A_2 = X_1A2‚Äč=X1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ X2=3X_2 = 3X2‚Äč=3 and B2=X2B_2 = X_2B2‚Äč=X2‚Äč &\&& X3=2X_3 = 2X3‚Äč=2.
For i=3i = 3i=3: A3=X1A_3 = X_1A3‚Äč=X1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ X2X_2X2‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ X3=3X_3 = 3X3‚Äč=3 and B3=X3=2B_3 = X_3 = 2B3‚Äč=X3‚Äč=2.

The XOR of all elements of XXX is 0‚äē3‚äē2=10\oplus 3\oplus 2 = 10‚äē3‚äē2=1. It can be proven that this is the maximum XOR value for any XXX.
Test case 222: An optimal good array is X=[2,1]X = [2, 1]X=[2,1].

For i=1i = 1i=1: A1=X1=2A_1 = X_1 = 2A1‚Äč=X1‚Äč=2 and B1=X1B_1 = X_1B1‚Äč=X1‚Äč &\&& X2=0X_2 = 0X2‚Äč=0.
For i=2i = 2i=2: A2=X1A_2 = X_1A2‚Äč=X1‚Äč ‚ą£|‚ą£ X2=3X_2 = 3X2‚Äč=3 and B2=X2=1B_2 = X_2 = 1B2‚Äč=X2‚Äč=1.

The XOR of all elements of XXX is 2‚äē1=32\oplus 1 = 32‚äē1=3. It can be proven that this is the maximum XOR value for any XXX.
